content: 2048, built in Python as part of Rice University's Principles of Computing Course. I designed the logic with instructions and Rice provided the GUI." target="2048">Click here to view the project repo on Github. I've also written a blog post exploring this project, the challenges I'm facing, and ideas I have for further exploration.

title: Basic Search Algorithm
content: I'm building a basic search algorithm in Python that allows a user to search an RSS feed from Craiglist. Click here to view the project repo on Github. I've also written a blog post exploring this project, the challenges I'm facing, and ideas I have for further exploration.

title: Tic Tac Toe...vs the Computer
content: The computer is not very smart yet, but it can prevent you from winning on the diagonals. In future versions, I will make the computer be able to block additional types of moves. It's eerie to see the game coming to life! You can change the size of the board on line 653, but keep the board a square larger than a 2x2 grid or else it will break. You can play the human-only version of the game here.

title: Blackjack
content: Blackjack! There are still a couple of bugs and this was built with a template that started with maybe 30% of the code already there, but it's still amazing to have made this.  There are a couple of bugs that need to be worked out, but it's mostly playable.  It feels really amazing to have built one of the old Windows card games.

title: Pong
content: This was a project for a Coursera  Class done in Python. Unfortunately, I lost the finished version, which kept score.  About 30% of the code was already provided in the template.
